body:
From what I understand, `${{ matrix.version == '1.12' }} && ${{ matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest' }}` is interpolated to `true && false` as an example. Unexpectedly, this is treated as a string instead of an expression. A non-empty string is treated as true so this if condition always evaluate to true.

I saw this error at https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCoupler.jl/actions/runs/22928949697.
title: Fix if condition for code coverage upload step

body:
This is a draft PR that updates the Coupler to ClimaAtmos. I still need to update the Manifest files after we do a release in Atmos. 

A quick question @juliasloan25 - Why are we doing this in line 103 of `ext/ClimaCouplerClimaAtmosExt.jl`
```
ᶜS_ρq_tot = integrator.p.precomputed.ᶜS_ρq_tot
 ᶜS_ρq_tot .= FT(0)
```
And also, the code below that computes the energy contribution is zero, because we set the source of mass to zero.
title: Update to new atmos

body: This PR fixes ~a bug with the manifest where an extension for Oceanigans did not exist and~ a bug with calibration where there was a missing qualifier for a function called from CalibrationTools.
title: Fix bug with calibration
